temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.effect: Raising temperature increases the reaction rate
temperature.detail: Van't Hoff rule: for every 10 °C rise, the rate increases 2–4 times. Particles move faster and collide more often with sufficient energy.

concentration.name: Concentration of reactants
concentration.effect: Increasing concentration increases the reaction rate
concentration.detail: More particles per unit volume means more frequent collisions. For gases: increasing pressure raises concentration.

surface_area.name: Surface area
surface_area.effect: Grinding a solid increases the reaction rate
surface_area.detail: More contact points between reactants. Example: iron powder reacts faster than a lump.

catalyst.name: Catalyst
catalyst.effect: Lowers activation energy, speeds up the reaction
catalyst.detail: A catalyst provides an alternative reaction pathway with lower activation energy. It is not consumed.

nature_of_reagents.name: Nature of reactants
nature_of_reagents.effect: Different substances react at different rates
nature_of_reagents.detail: Ionic reactions in solution occur almost instantaneously. Reactions involving covalent bond breaking are slower.

does_not_change: Amount of products when reaction goes to completion

temperature_increase.explanation: The system absorbs excess heat (Le Chatelier's principle)

temperature_decrease.explanation: The system releases heat to compensate

pressure_increase.explanation: The system tends to decrease pressure

pressure_decrease.explanation: The system tends to increase pressure

catalyst_added.explanation: A catalyst speeds up both the forward and reverse reactions equally

heat_classification.exothermic: Exothermic reaction — proceeds with release of heat (ΔH < 0)
heat_classification.endothermic: Endothermic reaction — proceeds with absorption of heat (ΔH > 0)
examples_exo: Reaction of active metals with water and acids
